req.title: System-Admin updates document
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to update a document through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates: (1) authenticated system-admin can update a document via PUT/PATCH /api/documents/{id}, (2) mandatory fields are validated, (3) an audit record is written on success, (4) HTTP 200/201 returned with entity id on success, (5) HTTP 400 on validation error, 401 on missing auth, 403 on wrong role. I selected five tests covering: EP-positive (valid admin update), NEG-unauthenticated (401), NEG-forbidden-role (403), NEG-missing-mandatory-field (400), BVA-title-at-max-length (boundary of a string field), and STC-audit-record-written (state transition from 'existing doc' to 'updated doc + audit'). Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up their own document.

tests.name: test_admin_update_document_valid_payload_returns_200
preconditions: A document with known ID exists
actions: Create a document via <create_document_fixture> to get document_id
Send PUT /api/documents/{document_id} with all mandatory fields populated
expected: Response body reflects updated field values
trace: R-WEB-030: expected outcomes – document is updated; response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"

def test_admin_update_document_valid_payload_returns_200():
    token = <get_admin_token>()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}

    doc_id = <create_document_fixture>(token)
    try:
        payload = {
            "title": "Updated Title",
            "content": "Updated content body",
            "status": "active"
        }
        resp = requests.put(
            f"{BASE_URL}/documents/{doc_id}",
            json=payload,
            headers=headers
        )
        assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status_code}"
        body = resp.json()
        assert str(body.get("id")) == str(doc_id)
        assert body.get("title") == "Updated Title"
    finally:
        <delete_document_fixture>(token, doc_id)

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_request_returns_401
preconditions: A document with known ID exists
actions: Send PUT /api/documents/{document_id} with no Authorization header
expected: Response body contains an error or message field indicating unauthorised
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"

def test_unauthenticated_request_returns_401():
    token = <get_admin_token>()
    doc_id = <create_document_fixture>(token)
    try:
        payload = {
            "title": "Any Title",
            "content": "Any content",
            "status": "active"
        }
        resp = requests.put(
            f"{BASE_URL}/documents/{doc_id}",
            json=payload
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}"
        body = resp.json()
        assert "error" in body or "message" in body
    finally:
        <delete_document_fixture>(token, doc_id)

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_returns_403
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role 'viewer' (not admin)
A document with known ID exists
actions: Send PUT /api/documents/{document_id} with valid payload but viewer token
expected: Response body contains error or message indicating forbidden
trace: R-WEB-030: error pathway – 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"

def test_forbidden_role_returns_403():
    admin_token = <get_admin_token>()
    viewer_token = <get_viewer_token>()
    doc_id = <create_document_fixture>(admin_token)
    try:
        headers = {
            "Authorization": f"Bearer {viewer_token}",
            "Content-Type": "application/json"
        }
        payload = {
            "title": "Viewer Attempt",
            "content": "Should be forbidden",
            "status": "active"
        }
        resp = requests.put(
            f"{BASE_URL}/documents/{doc_id}",
            json=payload,
            headers=headers
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}"
        body = resp.json()
        assert "error" in body or "message" in body
    finally:
        <delete_document_fixture>(admin_token, doc_id)

tests.name: test_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400
preconditions: A document with known ID exists
actions: Send PUT /api/documents/{document_id} omitting the mandatory 'title' field
expected: Response body references 'title' as a validation error
trace: R-WEB-030: mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-030: error pathway – 400 on validation error
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"

def test_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400():
    token = <get_admin_token>()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    doc_id = <create_document_fixture>(token)
    try:
        payload = {
            "content": "Content without title",
            "status": "active"
        }
        resp = requests.put(
            f"{BASE_URL}/documents/{doc_id}",
            json=payload,
            headers=headers
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 400, f"Expected 400, got {resp.status_code}"
        body = resp.json()
        errors = str(body)
        assert "title" in errors.lower()
    finally:
        <delete_document_fixture>(token, doc_id)

tests.name: test_title_at_max_length_boundary_accepted
preconditions: Title field has a defined max length of 255 characters
A document with known ID exists
actions: Send PUT /api/documents/{document_id} with title exactly 255 characters long
trace: R-WEB-030: mandatory fields must be validated – boundary of title length
R-WEB-030: expected outcomes – document is updated; response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"
MAX_TITLE_LEN = 255

def test_title_at_max_length_boundary_accepted():
    token = <get_admin_token>()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    doc_id = <create_document_fixture>(token)
    max_title = "A" * MAX_TITLE_LEN
    try:
        payload = {
            "title": max_title,
            "content": "Boundary test content",
            "status": "active"
        }
        resp = requests.put(
            f"{BASE_URL}/documents/{doc_id}",
            json=payload,
            headers=headers
        )
        assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status_code}"
        body = resp.json()
        assert str(body.get("id")) == str(doc_id)
        assert body.get("title") == max_title
    finally:
        <delete_document_fixture>(token, doc_id)

tests.name: test_successful_update_writes_audit_record
preconditions: A document with known ID exists
actions: Create a document via <create_document_fixture> to get document_id
Query audit log for records referencing document_id via <get_audit_records>
Verify audit record exists with correct action and actor
expected: Audit log contains at least one record for document_id
Audit record action indicates update (e.g. 'UPDATE' or 'update')
Audit record actor matches system-admin identity
trace: R-WEB-030: expected outcomes – audit record written on success
R-WEB-030: state transition from 'document exists' to 'document updated + audit written'
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"

def test_successful_update_writes_audit_record():
    token = <get_admin_token>()
    admin_identity = <get_admin_identity>()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    doc_id = <create_document_fixture>(token)
    try:
        payload = {
            "title": "Audit Check Title",
            "content": "Audit check content",
            "status": "active"
        }
        resp = requests.put(
            f"{BASE_URL}/documents/{doc_id}",
            json=payload,
            headers=headers
        )
        assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status_code}"
        audit_records = <get_audit_records>(token, entity_id=doc_id)
        assert len(audit_records) >= 1, "No audit records found for document"
        actions = [r.get("action", "").upper() for r in audit_records]
        assert any("UPDATE" in a for a in actions), f"No UPDATE audit action found: {actions}"
        actors = [r.get("actor", "") for r in audit_records]
        assert any(admin_identity in a for a in actors), "Admin actor not in audit record"
    finally:
        <delete_document_fixture>(token, doc_id)
